actions.reason: reference sub +14.2 dB vs input mix +17.8 dB (delta -3.7 dB)
reference low +7.5 dB vs input mix +11.1 dB (delta -3.6 dB)
reference air -28.8 dB vs input mix -26.8 dB (delta -2.0 dB)
reference lowmid +6.4 dB vs input mix +8.4 dB (delta -2.0 dB)

bus_balance.note: Dry input balance is diagnostic only. Render-time bus gains align post-FX vocal/accomp active-region RMS to the reference stems.

actions.reason: reference vocal low -15.7 dB vs input vocal -12.0 dB (delta -3.7 dB)
reference vocal lowmid +6.5 dB vs input vocal +9.3 dB (delta -2.8 dB)

skipped.reason: harsh/sibilance evidence is not safe enough for high-frequency boost
harsh/sibilance evidence is not safe enough for high-frequency boost

vocal_eq.policy: match current dry vocal tonal shape toward the reference, but gate upper/air boosts by template and harsh/sibilance safety evidence; 14k air is conservative

actions.reason: input active vocal gap -4.8 dB trails reference -1.4 dB; current upper masking +5.0 dB vs reference -4.3 dB (excess +9.3 dB)
input active vocal gap -4.8 dB trails reference -1.4 dB; current lowmid masking -3.1 dB vs reference -5.5 dB (excess +2.4 dB)

duck_coordination.policy: one static carve per spectral problem region; matching dynamic duck bands are reduced, but strong reference-relative masking preserves more dynamic duck so a small carve does not pretend the masking problem is solved

accomp_eq.policy: cut only; one carve per problem region, coordinated with vocal-aware ducking

dry_vocal_strategy.policy: Use dry vocal spectral shape to decide where accompaniment yields; overall vocal/accomp gain still follows the reference active ratio conservatively.

important_note: A/B/C now render through template-specific Faust approximation chains. Default backend is native Faust shell rendering. Use --render-backend wasm only for development smoke checks, or --legacy-current-renderer to force the older full_fx_mix.sh path.
